query: Detourmap complaints OR frustrating OR broken

results.title: Trip Reviews | Detours Travel
results.content:
A special shout-out to our guide, Jared, who truly made the trip a joy. He was patient, approachable, and consistently upbeat - always ready with helpful tips, entertaining stories, and a contagious enthusiasm that brought our group together. Jared’s calm organization behind the scenes meant we never had to worry about logistics, and his fun, welcoming personality kept everyone smiling.  
     
   I had zero complaints - everything ran smoothly, the group dynamic was amazing, and the itinerary showcased the best of Scandinavia. I would absolutely travel with Detours again and highly recommend them to anyone seeking a well-planned yet flexible adventure.
 #### Scott B. - USA [...] He instantly wins everyone over with his amazing energetic, positive, humorous, welcoming, kind, warm, personality he knows his business inside and out nothing stumps him he always comes thru even after hours.  
   What a beyond excellent representative for Detours Travel  
   Bravo Brandon!!!!!  
     
   The trip every detail looked after amazing transfers, hotels, restaurants, scheduled and extra tours offered ( no hard sell just suggestions you may want to pursue or not )  
     
   Any of my friends knows I am a tough demanding customer with high standards.  
   I can honestly say all were met no complaints whatsoever ever I would go on another Detours adventure for sure maybe only travel this way from now on  
     
   Two more things I will share: [...] I was recently on the February 2016 12-day trip to Costa Rica and was thrilled with the experience that Detours provided. The outstanding customer service started long before the trip, when I contacted Miles Mitchinson with a number of questions; he was always prompt to reply and provide information. And right up until the start of the trip, Miles was always there with an answer, and even an e-mail greeting, upon arrival in Costa Rica. Our tour guide, Dan Clarke, was amazing; definitely, the best tour guide I’ve ever had the pleasure to spend my vacation with. Oh so knowledgeable, he guided us through Costa Rica, with broken Spanish ;-), the entire 12 days. By far, though, the best part of the trip to Costa Rica was the experience that was provided. Miles and the Detours’ team have

results.title: Detour Insurance | Customer Reviews
results.content:
\The overall customer rating is the average of all reviews for the provider since they joined the marketplace.

## Customer Reviews

Detour customers highlight the provider’s efficient claims process, with some getting claims settled faster than expected. Other travelers, however, report frustrations at the amount of supporting documentation requested when submitting a claim.

Filter
Sort

Unfortunately it has been six weeks and my claim has still not been processed.

Sounds like you are all set, Kayleigh. Our Claims team reported that your claim was approved this week, and your refund has been processed. We hope you will consider Detour to protect future trips.

Overall good experience and fair pricing [...] Christiane,
  
We're sorry to hear you're frustrated with the claims process. Our goal is always to review
  
and resolve claims as quickly as possible. In your case, additional information was needed to continue processing the claim, and requests were sent on April 15 and April 25. On April 27, you advised our claims team that the information would be provided. A follow-up email was also sent to you on May 12. At this time, we are still awaiting the requested documentation needed to continue the review process. We remain committed to helping move the claim forward as soon as the requested information is received. Please reference the emails previously sent to you and contact our claims team directly so we can assist further. Thank you.

I remain confused about coverage [...] Hi Stacy,
  
Thanks for taking time to share your feedback. We're glad we were able to assist with your policy update, and we appreciate you choosing Detour. Happy to help anytime.

Ins claimed that plane malfunction and cancellation not covered. We were unable to get to Iceland before cruise left.

I submitted a claim with with Detour 16 days ago regarding a trip interruption and a lost/delayed bag. So far I have received ZERO communication or feedback.

Great assistance from Detour underwriter as I had to change the coverage amount several times.

While we did not need to use the Detour policy, it was reassuring to know that we had it just in case. The customer service was fine and the cost is fair. What more could you ask for,

Last updated: July 19, 2026

Squaremouth logo

results.title: Bug: Map routes are often completely broken - Console Discussion - Diablo IV Forums
results.content:
Outsids those areas, it actually works pretty well from what I’ve seen.

For me, the map route problems are constant — definitely can call it “often”. The changing-map theory may be right, but it doesn’t do anything to actually fix the problem.

Having completed the campaign a couple of times I have to say that this is not really a thing.

Though I did have some issues when I placed a marker in a blank area and when I got closer the marker was on a solid inaccessible piece of rock.

Powered by Discourse, best viewed with JavaScript enabled [...] # Bug: Map routes are often completely broken

On Xbox, map routes are often completely broken. See imgur dot com /a/gYbNV0L for example screenshots.

Half the time, the route shown to a location/marker is not traversable — it goes through solid rock, across non-accessible areas of the map, to the back of a building, etc. Even when passible, a route is often nonsensical — e.g., wildly out of the way and 3 times longer than it needs to be.

Not really “often”. It’s certain areas. Caldeum and Tree of Whispers as well.

So Caldeum, Tree of Whispers, the desert you showed here, and the other place you’re showing.

What do these all have in common? The maps are changed during the campaign, I’m guessing that’s why these areas have issues.

results.title: Detour Maps | FAQ's
results.content:
Detour’s printed map is a way to have everything there is to know about the community in the palm of your hand. With 2 publications each year for each community and over 40,000 maps distributed you will be well informed with our printed maps. Inside the printed maps include a map of the community with different local business advertisements. The map pin points each business giving the user a better idea of where the business or resource is located in the community.We do not just have business locations on the map; we also have locations of parks, bus routes, schools, useful phone numbers, events and basically all public assets in the community. The printed map also is color coded so that residents may know which business is participating in the $10 Savings Card promotion. [...] ## For the online user

### General Questions about the website:

### What is detourmaps.com?

Detourmaps.com is a community based interactive marketing website that inspires buying local through a collective local business advertising platform. This platform helps local businesses connect with neighborhoods and organizations.

With Detour Maps, you can browse and explore places within your community. You can: [...] Detour offers a number of distinctive products and services that work together to create full circle direct marketing support, regardless of how dispersed the audience might be. (Solutions would be print solutions, internet solutions etc.)

results.title: My serious list of Issues with Roadcraft
results.content: The game feels incredibly shallow, offers no real rewards, the implementation of most mechanics are mediocre at best and utterly terrible at worst.
